li.p: To find the y-intercept, compute g(0)={3\mathopen{}\left(0+2\right)\mathopen{}\left(0-4\right)}=-24\text{.} So the y-intercept is {\left(0,-24\right)}\text{.}
The quadratic function is given in factored form. We can see that subbing in x=-2 or x=4 would result in the output being 0\text{.} So the x-intercepts are (-2, 0) and (4, 0)\text{.}
